Title: Feruloylation and structure of arabinoxylan in wheat endosperm cell walls from RNAi lines with suppression of genes responsible for backbone synthesis and decoration

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan/xylan biosynthesis in wheat endosperm cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce total and water-extractable arabinoxylan in white flour and alter cell-wall architecture; could be used to modify processing quality, feed/distilling properties, and dietary-fibre composition. Because suppression causes ~40–50% AX reduction and shorter chains while diferulate levels are relatively maintained, deployment should consider effects on wall thickness/integrity and end-use viscosity; stacking with decoration/feruloylation genes may be needed for precise fibre tailoring.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a xylan synthase complex component required for synthesis/elongation of the xylan backbone; exact catalytic chemistry is not directly assigned in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi suppression decreases total, water-extractable, and xylanase-extractable arabinoxylan by about 40–50%, with smaller effects on ferulate and especially diferulate, increasing diferulate per unit AX and preserving highly feruloylated xylanase-unextractable AX.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression in wheat; monosaccharide/HPAEC quantification of AX; sequential fractionation into WE/XE/XU fractions; HPLC quantification of bound ferulate and diferulates; 1H-NMR analysis of arabinosylation patterns; prior structural evidence on shorter AX chains cited from related transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for synthesis of the target polysaccharide backbone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Grain endosperm development; white flour/endosperm cell wall context; transgenic plants grown in glasshouse, wild type in field
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Putative xylan synthase complex component GT43
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in wheat endosperm under HMW promoter-driven RNAi; endogenous expression pattern not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats TaGT43_2 as encoding a component of xylan synthase rather than proving a standalone catalytic activity. Suppression affects WE-AX and XE-AX strongly but XU-AX only weakly, indicating wall fractions differ in resilience and feruloylation. Effects are interpreted alongside earlier work showing reduced AX chain length.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Xylan backbone synthesis module of endosperm arabinoxylan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced AX content and thinner endosperm cell walls may risk altered grain texture or wall integrity; compensatory increases in ferulate dimerization suggest structural stress responses.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for AX accumulation, supported by strong reduction of AX after suppression; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGT43_2 suppression reduced total and soluble/endoxylanase-extractable arabinoxylan in wheat endosperm by about 40–50%. 2. Ferulate monomer fell less than AX and diferulate was relatively maintained, causing increased diferulate per unit AX and higher apparent cross-linking. 3. The highly feruloylated xylanase-unextractable AX fraction was largely preserved, supporting a model that wall-integrity demands maintain diferulate levels when AX backbone synthesis is compromised.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wheat grain starchy endosperm cell walls / white flour
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary fibre component; influences food processing quality, extract viscosity, feed/distilling quality, and cell-wall structural integrity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan/xylan biosynthesis in wheat endosperm cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable target for engineering lower AX content and altered endosperm cell-wall properties in wheat flour. Suitable for modifying fibre quantity and functionality, but because suppression reduces AX markedly and likely shortens chains while wall cross-linking is maintained, breeding use should monitor end-use quality and structural penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a xylan synthase complex component required for synthesis/elongation of the xylan backbone; exact catalytic chemistry is not directly demonstrated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi suppression decreases total, WE-, and XE-arabinoxylan by about 40–50%, while ferulate and especially diferulate decrease less, leading to increased diferulate per unit AX and maintenance of highly feruloylated XU-AX.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression in wheat; HPAEC monosaccharide/AX analysis; sequential WE/XE/XU fractionation; HPLC of bound ferulate and diferulates; 1H-NMR of AX substitution patterns.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for synthesis of the target polysaccharide backbone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Grain endosperm development; white flour/endosperm cell wall context; glasshouse-grown transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Putative xylan synthase complex component GT47
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in wheat endosperm under HMW promoter-driven RNAi; endogenous expression pattern not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: As with TaGT43_2, the study identifies TaGT47_2 as a xylan synthase component on the basis of RNAi phenotypes rather than direct enzyme biochemistry. Effects are strongest in low- and moderately feruloylated fractions and minimal in the highly feruloylated XU fraction.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Xylan backbone synthesis module of endosperm arabinoxylan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential reduction in wall thickness/integrity and altered flour functionality due to strong AX depletion; compensatory cross-linking changes may create secondary quality effects.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for AX accumulation, supported by strong suppression phenotype; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGT47_2 suppression caused major reductions in wheat endosperm arabinoxylan content across total, WE, and XE fractions. 2. Ferulate monomer decreased less than AX and diferulate was comparatively preserved, increasing diferulate per unit AX. 3. The XU-AX fraction remained largely unaffected, supporting the idea that heavily feruloylated/cross-linked AX is preferentially maintained to preserve wall function.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wheat grain starchy endosperm cell walls / white flour
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary fibre component; affects food processing, viscosity, feed/distilling uses, and structural properties of endosperm cell walls

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Arabinoxylan side-chain decoration / arabinosylation in wheat endosperm cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to alter AX substitution pattern and feruloylation distribution without large reductions in total AX, enabling finer control of dietary fibre functionality and extract viscosity. Especially useful for editing mono-arabinosylation on xylan; however, compensation by increased di-substitution in XE-AX and maintenance of diferulate-rich XU-AX mean outcomes may be fraction-specific.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers arabinofuranose onto the xylan backbone, particularly generating arabinose 3-linked to mono-substituted xylose residues; the study suggests it may preferentially transfer non-feruloylated Araf.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi suppression causes only small decreases in total AX (0–15%) but strongly reduces mono-substituted AX oligosaccharides/A3-Xmono, greatly lowers feruloylation in WE-AX, leaves XE-AX AX amount largely unchanged, and is accompanied by increased di-substitution in XE-AX.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific RNAi suppression in wheat; HPAEC analysis of AX oligosaccharides; 1H-NMR quantification of A3-Xmono, A3-Xdi, and A2-Xdi signals; HPLC quantification of bound ferulate and diferulates in WE/XE/XU fractions; monosaccharide-based AX measurements.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for side-chain decoration that enables/conditions feruloylation of the target polysaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Grain endosperm development; white flour/endosperm cell wall context; glasshouse-grown transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reported as highest in starchy endosperm in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in starchy endosperm (as discussed for TaXAT1)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes that suppressing TaXAT1 does not proportionally reduce feruloylation in XE-AX despite reduced A3-Xmono, implying TaXAT1 may transfer mainly non-feruloylated Araf and that feruloylated Araf addition may involve another transfer route. In XE-AX, di-substitution increases 30–40%, offsetting loss of mono-substitution.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Arabinose side-chain addition to xylan; mono-substituted xylose decoration module
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can shift AX substitution pattern in a fraction-specific way, potentially causing unexpected compensation in di-substitution and only limited change in total AX; effects on end-use traits may therefore be less predictable than backbone-gene suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Arabinose-substituted xylan residues, especially Araf 3-linked to mono-substituted Xylp
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for AX arabinosylation pattern, especially mono-substitution; not established as rate-limiting for total AX accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaXAT1 suppression had modest effects on total AX quantity but strongly reduced Araf 3-linked to mono-substituted xylose in wheat endosperm AX. 2. WE-AX became much less feruloylated, whereas XE-AX retained ferulate/diferulate and showed increased di-substitution, indicating strong fraction-specific compensation. 3. The results support a model in which TaXAT1 primarily transfers non-feruloylated arabinose rather than the feruloylated Araf species used for AX feruloylation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Wheat grain starchy endosperm cell walls / white flour
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary fibre component affecting processing quality, viscosity, and cell-wall structure; its feruloylated form contributes to cross-linking and wall integrity
